programmes.show_description: Gamer Lauren is looking for her perfect player number two to help her reboot her love life. And Ryan is searching for a silver fox with a dad bod.
Britain's sharpest armchair critics have been watching: The Traitors, Gino's Cooking Up Love, Lady Chatterley's Lover, I Can See Your Voice, Blowing LA and White Christmas.
Can Kenny and his biceps 'James' and 'Andrew' sweep gym manager Claire off her feet? And will the stars align for former Countdown contestant Jonny and mystical Clare?
The Bank Job: Peralta and Diaz learn that there's more to Lt Melanie Hawkins' team than meets the eye as they hunt for the gang of bank robbers.
Crime & Punishment: Peralta and Diaz are framed for a crime they didn't commit. Can their Nine-Nine buddies save them from jail!?
The Greatest Musical Ever Written: Barry makes fun of Adam for taking part in a school musical. Beverly's job as a substitute teacher causes problems for Erica.
Globetrotters: When Adam creates a video that makes it look as though he and his friends beat Barry at basketball, Barry demands a rematch in the gym.
Bride Lauren and music-mad Perry are under the spotlight as they look back on their gig-themed wedding which Perry threw at a popular London music venue. (Ep5)
The Heist: The teams each build their own portion of a city-wide heist, in assorted heist opportunities including a bank, an art gallery, a hotel and the docks.
Rude Tube's naughty little sister presents a man pretending to be a walrus, an incredible downhill bike run and some computer keyboard hurling.
The Aussie Soap One: Australia-born Adam is taking a risk planning an '80s Aussie soap' wedding when his bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated...
Truth Be Told: After reconnecting on Facebook, Phil invites his old high school girlfriend to drop by the house, but the friendly visit quickly turns into trouble.
Starry Night: Jay and Mitchell set out on their traditional father-son outing to see the meteor showers, but Mitchell is surprised to see that Manny will be joining them.
Game Changer: Mitchell decides to toughen up, Cameron gets involved in a neighbour's marital problems, and it's Jay against Gloria and Manny in chess.
Benched: When Luke and Manny's hot-tempered basketball coach quits in the middle of a game, Jay and Phil both jockey to fill the position.
Grasshopper Also: In Carlstadt, New Jersey, Gordon Ramsay attempts to save the Grasshopper Also, a family-owned Irish restaurant that's on its last legs.
Davide: In Boston, Gordon Ramsay visits Davide, an Italian restaurant that's been run into the ground by brothers Frank and Anthony. Can they learn to trust each other again?
Cafe Tavolini: In Bridgeport, Connecticut, Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe Tavolini, a restaurant that's being neglected as the couple who run it teeter on the edge of divorce.
Zeke's: In New Orleans, Gordon Ramsay visits a restaurant famed for its Cajun food and loyal locals. But under new ownership, standards have slipped and the customers have left.
Lost Verizon: When Bart finds Denis Leary's phone and uses it to make prank calls and accept job offers, Denis comes up with a creative punishment for him.
Homer and Lisa Exchange Cross Words: Lisa discovers that she has a knack for crosswords and enters a tournament. But Homer thinks he can make money by betting on her to lose.
Mypods and Broomsticks: Homer suspects that Bart's new friend's Muslim family are terrorists. Meanwhile, Lisa becomes addicted to downloading songs on her new Mypod.
Take My Life, Please: Homer is annoyed when his old high school nemesis Vance Connor gets a spot on the Springfield Walk of Fame.
Deep Space Homer: NASA recruit Homer and Barney to make the organisation more appealing to the common man. When Barney has too much to drink, Homer is chosen to go into space.
Homer Loves Flanders: Homer realises that he likes Flanders and starts to hang around with him all the time. Unfortunately, Flanders realises that he's not so keen on Homer...
Bart Gets an Elephant: Bart wins an elephant, but Homer can't afford its upkeep and decides to sell it to an ivory dealer...
Burns' Heir: When Bart wins a competition to become Mr Burns' heir, he must choose between his boring family and the rich, over-indulgent power plant owner...
Sweet Seymour Skinner's Baadasssss Song: Bart gets Principal Skinner sacked and Flanders takes over at the school. Out of work, Skinner befriends his erstwhile nemesis Bart.
(2017) Hugh Jackman, Zac Efron and Michelle Williams star in the Golden-Globe-winning, toe-tapping smash hit musical about the life and times of P.T. Barnum, who created the circus.
A bunch of famous faces watch The Piano, Bridgerton, Celebrity Bridge of Lies, UK's Strongest Man 2024, The World's Most Luxurious Retirement Homes and Scam Interceptors.
What do the Goggleboxers think of Blue Lights, Strictly, Victoria Beckham (on Netflix), Slow Horses, Antiques Roadshow, Eva Longoria: Searching for Spain and Police Interceptors?
This time on the 'Box, The Celebrity Traitors, Strictly Come Dancing, Hollyoaks, Dragons' Den, Beat the Chasers, Celebrity Help! My House Is Haunted and BBC News (Prince Andrew).
Can Kenny and his biceps 'James' and 'Andrew' sweep gym manager Claire off her feet? And will the stars align for former Countdown contestant Jonny and mystical Clare?
Will romance blossom between glam Rachel and unlucky-in-love Alan, as they discuss her experiences of living with OCD? And will Jess like Ross's cheesy jokes more than Regan did?
What do the Goggleboxers think of Blue Lights, Strictly, Victoria Beckham (on Netflix), Slow Horses, Antiques Roadshow, Eva Longoria: Searching for Spain and Police Interceptors?
This time on the 'Box, The Celebrity Traitors, Strictly Come Dancing, Hollyoaks, Dragons' Den, Beat the Chasers, Celebrity Help! My House Is Haunted and BBC News (Prince Andrew).
The Aussie Soap One: Australia-born Adam is taking a risk planning an '80s Aussie soap' wedding when his bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated...
Finale (Part 2/2): In the last ever episode, the entire family discover that saying goodbye is much harder than it seems.
The Bicycle Thief: Phil is determined to help Luke show his mum that he's responsible enough to have the new bike they just got him, but Phil ends up being the irresponsible one.
After months of torment, Dodger finally has his chance to expose Sully, but Sully's ready to fight dirty... Vicky bonds with Prince, Sienna makes a tough decision and Donny faces the truth.
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister that features a variety of cats doing strange things, some soldiers stuck in the sand and a dog balancing on a rope.
Truth Be Told: After reconnecting on Facebook, Phil invites his old high school girlfriend to drop by the house, but the friendly visit quickly turns into trouble.
SuperShowerBabyBowl: Jay is hosting a Super Bowl party, while Claire hosts a baby shower for Haley - and they're both at Jay's house. What could possibly go wrong?
Red Alert: Lily has reached a womanly milestone that Cam and Mitch are not at all prepared for. So they call in reinforcements...
The Wild: Mitch, Cam and Phil crash Jay's annual hiking trip to find an elusive bald eagle. And Gloria, Alex, Claire and Haley get together to plan a baby shower.
Stand by Your Man: Dylan and Luke are impressed by Bill's tales of heroism as a firefighter, which makes Phil feel emasculated. So he invites the guys over for male bonding.
Ben Shephard chats about new mountaineering challenge The Summit. Ruby Wax talks about her tour. Plus: Emmett J Scanlan, Mollie King, Matt Edmondson, Dr Sian Williams and Joshua Idehen.
Cheeky original clips featuring ducks going up an escalator, animal impressions, a few narrow escapes on a snowboard and rally driving. Plus: an energy drink house prank and a butcher rap.
Gordon Returns 2: Gordon Ramsay revisits two restaurants - Giuseppi's and Sante La Brea - to find out whether his advice has been put to good use.
Down City: Gordon Ramsay tries to save struggling restaurant Down City in Providence, Rhode Island. But tough-talking owner Abby refuses to take advice from anyone.
(1990) Action comedy with Arnold Schwarzenegger. A tough cop goes undercover as a kindergarten teacher. Tough? Ha! The kids'll take him down in no time.
The Boy Who Knew Too Much: Bart is witness when Mayor Quimby's nephew is falsely accused of assaulting a French butler. But to testify, he has to admit to skipping school...
Lady Bouvier's Lover: Grampa and Mr Burns compete for the affections of Marge's mum.
Secrets of a Successful Marriage: Homer teaches a marriage guidance class but divulges a load of secrets about Marge and ends up in the doghouse. Or the tree house, at least.
The City of New York vs. Homer Simpson: Homer must pluck up the courage to return to New York after Barney leaves his car there on a drunken binge.
The Principal and the Pauper: The real Seymour Skinner (Martin Sheen) returns to Springfield, revealing that Principal Skinner is an impostor whose real name is Armin Tamzarian.
Treehouse of Horror XXXII: Another Halloween helping of spooky tales, featuring some chilling takes on classic films including Parasite, Nightmare on Elm Street and, er, Bambi.
The Wayz We Were: Evergreen Terrace becomes one giant traffic jam thanks to a new GPS app. Moe reunites with an old flame - and this time he's determined to not screw it up.
(2002) Action-thriller starring Jason Statham. For a price, an underworld courier will deliver people or packages anywhere, no questions asked. But one job lands him in deep danger.
Self-professed 'crazy camel lady' Sandra is after a mature man to ride off into the sunset with. Pansexual erotic writer Toni-Ann dreams of being the heroine in her own sexy novel.
The Goggleboxers watch Starstruck, The Piano, You, Rick Stein's Cornwall, Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over USA, This Morning (longest tongue record) and ITV News of a cheese controversy.
